title: Concerning the National Collegiate Athletic Association policy for eligibility in women's sports.
plain_summary: This House resolution asks the NCAA to change its rules about who can play in women's sports. Right now, the NCAA allows some transgender student athletes to compete in women's sports. The House wants the NCAA to create new rules that only let people who were born as biological females play in women's sports. The resolution also asks all sports organizations to protect women's sports for biological women and girls only.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.

sections.title: Preamble - Whereas Clauses
sections.text: This resolution states several facts and beliefs about women's sports. It says that playing sports helps young girls become healthier and more confident. It notes that women have achieved great things in American sports, from the Olympics to professional teams, especially after getting more chances to play in college sports. The resolution mentions Title Nine, a law from nineteen seventy-two that requires schools receiving federal money to treat male and female students equally in sports programs. The resolution claims there are basic biological differences between males and females that make it unfair and unsafe for females to compete against males in sports. It states that in two thousand ten, the National Collegiate Athletic Association (a group that oversees college sports called the NCAA) created a policy allowing biological males to join women's teams and compete in women's sports categories. The resolution says this policy hurts female athletes. It notes that another college sports organization called the National Association of Intercollegiate Athletics has made new rules to protect biological women in sports by only allowing students whose biological sex is female to compete on women's teams. The resolution says it is very important to protect opportunities for college women to compete in sports based on their sex. It states that NCAA member schools must follow Title Nine to ensure equal benefits and opportunities in sports programs based on sex.

sections.title: Resolution Demands
sections.text: The House of Representatives makes four specific demands in this resolution. First, it asks the NCAA to cancel its current policy about transgender student athletes because the House believes this policy unfairly treats female student athletes differently. Second, it strongly urges the NCAA to immediately protect college women's sports by not allowing transgender-identifying males to compete on any women's sports teams or in any college competitions. Third, it asks the NCAA to require all its member conferences to follow a policy based on biological sex for all sports and all divisions. Fourth, it calls on all organizations that govern sports in the United States to protect women's sports categories for biological women and girls only.
